description: All rough electrical wiring, boxes, panels, and service equipment are installed per approved plans and NEC/Baldwin County requirements, rough-in inspection is passed, and no walls are authorized to close until superintendent sign-off is complete.

categories.name: Plans & Pre-Start Verification
items: Approved electrical permit card posted on-site and visible to inspector
Stamped electrical plan set on-site and matches current approved revision
Electrician has reviewed final lighting fixture selections and confirmed box locations, weights, and fan-rated boxes for all ceiling fan locations
Home-run circuit schedule reviewed against client's finalized appliance and equipment list (EV charger, pool equipment, generator, wine cooler, etc.)
Superintendent has confirmed all low-voltage rough-in trades (structured wiring, security, A/V) are coordinated with electrical so conduit and boxes don't conflict

categories.name: Service & Panel Rough-In
items: Temporary power relocated or permanent service entrance location confirmed with Alabama Power and clearance distances verified
Main panel location confirmed per plan; panel is plumb, secured to backing, and accessible per NEC 110.26 working-space requirements (min. 36" depth, 30" width, 6'6" headroom)
Panel schedule posted inside panel door and matches circuit layout on approved plans
Sub-panel locations (garage, pool house, detached structure) roughed in with correct wire gauge and conduit per load calculations on approved plans
Grounding electrode system installed: ground rods driven, Ufer/concrete-encased electrode confirmed with electrician before slab pour if applicable, all bonding conductors sized and connected
Service entrance conduit and meter can set; Alabama Power meter base requirements confirmed (200A or 400A service as designed)

categories.name: Rough-In Wiring
items: All branch circuit wiring is correct gauge for circuit amperage: 15A = 14 AWG, 20A = 12 AWG, 30A/40A/50A dedicated circuits sized per load schedule
All boxes are installed at consistent heights per Palmetto standard (switches 48" AFF, receptacles 16" AFF, or as noted on plans) and secured without play or wobble
All outlet spacing meets NEC 210.52: no point along a wall is more than 6 feet from a receptacle; kitchen countertop receptacles within 2 feet of countertop edge and no more than 4 feet apart
All bathroom circuits are 20A dedicated per NEC 210.11(C)(3); GFCI protection locations confirmed for baths, kitchen, garage, exterior, and within 6 feet of any sink
Dedicated 20A kitchen small-appliance circuits (minimum two) roughed in above countertops per NEC 210.52(B)
All high-draw appliances on dedicated circuits: refrigerator (20A), dishwasher (20A), microwave (20A), range/oven (per spec), washer (20A), dryer (30A or 240V per spec), HVAC disconnects at units
All wire runs secured with staples or straps within 12" of boxes and every 4.5 feet along runs; no loose wire draped through framing
All wire passing through studs and plates is protected by nail plates where within 1.25" of face of stud per NEC 300.4

categories.name: Specialty & Custom Systems
items: Generator transfer switch or interlock kit roughed in at main panel location per client's generator spec; conduit to generator pad stubbed out
EV charging circuit (minimum 50A/240V, or as specified) roughed in to garage with correct conduit and wire gauge
Outdoor kitchen, pool, and spa circuits roughed in to correct locations; all outdoor and pool circuits confirmed for GFCI/AFCI requirements per NEC Article 680
Whole-home surge protection provision made at main panel (device or mounting space reserved per spec)
All ceiling fan locations confirmed with fan-rated boxes and blocking; boxes rated for the fan weight specified in lighting selections
Under-cabinet lighting rough-in (junction boxes or low-voltage transformer locations) confirmed against cabinet shop drawings
Smart-home / lighting-control rough-in (Lutron, Crestron, or specified system) coordinated: multi-gang boxes, neutral wires pulled to all switch locations as required by system spec

categories.name: Quality & Code Checks
items: AFCI protection confirmed for all required locations per NEC 210.12: bedrooms, living rooms, dining rooms, hallways, and all 15A/20A 120V circuits in dwelling areas
All junction boxes are accessible (not buried in insulation or behind permanent surfaces) and covered with blank plates
No wire splices outside of listed junction boxes; all splices in boxes with correct wire connectors
Smoke detector rough-in locations verified per NFPA 72 and Alabama Residential Code: every level, inside and outside each sleeping area, interconnected circuit pulled
Carbon monoxide detector rough-in locations confirmed per Alabama code: outside each sleeping area if home has attached garage or fuel-burning appliances
Superintendent has walked entire rough-in with lead electrician and confirmed no box locations conflict with cabinet layout, crown molding runs, or tile shower walls per finalized selections
All wire labels or circuit identifiers are written at the panel end before cover-up

categories.name: Inspection & Pre-Cover Sign-Off
items: Baldwin County electrical rough-in inspection scheduled and confirmation number on file
Baldwin County rough-in inspection passed; inspection card signed and posted
Any corrections noted on inspection report completed and re-inspection passed before framing cover-up begins
Superintendent has conducted independent walk with Palmetto's quality checklist before calling for inspection — no open items remaining
Electrician has confirmed all home-run labels at panel are complete and panel schedule is up to date with any field changes documented as a change order if scope changed
Signed pre-cover authorization issued by superintendent to framing/insulation crew; no walls to be closed until this sign-off is in the project file
